home_read.momentum: Came from behind to win 3-2 with the last kick, giving their tournament a positive ending despite a disastrous campaign.

tactical_shape: Pochettino used a more familiar-looking shape with Sergiño Dest and Alex Freeman as positional alternatives to Joe Scally, and later checked in Dest to pin Turkey back.
Full-backs bore a greater responsibility for progression than the central routes.
standout_players: Matt Turner 4/10 — unable to stop the three shots he faced, though he made a couple of timely sweeper actions.
Joe Scally 5/10 — struggled to keep up with the game and was pulled out of position twice on Turkey’s second goal.
Mark McKenzie 5/10 — bypassed too easily for Turkey’s first goal, but helped funnel the ball into midfield.
Miles Robinson 5/10 — looked unready early, then settled.
Auston Trusty 7/10 — headed home the opening goal, helped in build-up and tracking back, but exited with an apparent left ankle injury.
Sebastian Berhalter 8/10 — assist for Trusty’s opener, scored, and was the team’s most progressive passer.
Weston McKennie 7/10 — led as captain and helped keep spark under teammates.
Gio Reyna 5/10 — moved a lot to offer a passing option but recirculated more than he broke lines.
Tim Weah 5/10 — too many errant passes, sloppy touches and ineffective dribbles.
Brenden Aaronson 5/10 — all industry, but missed an open-net chance.
Ricardo Pepi 5/10 — dragged centre-backs deeper but struggled to find box touches.
Christian Pulisic 6/10 — looked ready, nearly scored from distance and carried threat quickly after coming on.
Alex Freeman 5/10 — caught out of position on the late winner.
Sergiño Dest 6/10 — pinned Turkey back with his cameo.
Alejandro Zendejas 6/10 — little to work with in a quiet debut.
Malik Tillman 6/10 — brought on late to balance the midfield split.
fatigue_injuries: Auston Trusty exited with an apparent left ankle injury.
Christian Pulisic was not fully in the expected starting rhythm but showed few signs of rust.
Cristian Roldan was injured, prompting McKennie to go again.
away_read.momentum: Despite the late defeat, the US had already topped the group and move into the knockout stage with confidence and some fresh questions after wilting in the decisive moments.
